rationale: The citing paper uses Cai, Shen, and Hui (2012) in its core theory section as direct evidence that spatial location can shift numerical judgments (price estimates). This evidence is used to support the paper's proposed mental number-line mapping mechanism and the claim that number-location association is bidirectional, which motivates the rank length hypotheses. Although the citation is not tied to method reuse, it plays a meaningful conceptual role in framing and mechanism justification rather than being a passing literature-list mention.
relation_description: The citing paper treats Cai et al. (2012) as prior empirical support that spatial cues affect numeric estimation, and uses that result as a conceptual basis for its own rank-length mapping account. In this sense, the cited study underpins the mechanism-level framing of the new theory.
